name: Laza, Irati/Abodi, Port de Larrau – Circular Route
name_original: Laza, Irati/Abodi, Port de Larrau – Ruta Circular
region_intro: Hiking around Uztárroz/Uztarroze offers access to diverse natural landscapes within the Pyrenees, characterized by mountainous terrain, dense forests, and significant natural features. The region includes high peaks like Pic d'Orhy and mountain passes such as Larrau Pass. Gorges like Olhadubi, with its Holzarte Footbridge, and waterfalls such as Belabarzeko Urjausia, add to the varied topography. The area provides a network of trails through valleys and woodlands, catering to different hiking preferences. Best hiking trails around Uztárroz/Uztarroze The most popular hiking route is Pic d'Orhy Trail from Port de Larrau, a difficult 2.7 miles (4.4 km) trail that takes 2 hours 25 minutes to complete. This route ascends to Pic d'Orhy, offering panoramic views of the Pyrenees. Another top favourite among local hikers is Artikomendia Trail, a moderate 3.4 miles (5.5 km) path. This trail winds through the Roncal Valley, featuring Romanesque bridges and the Cueva del Ibón. Local hikers also love the Belabartsaitsa Trail, a 3.7 miles (6.0 km) trail leading through forested mountain terrain, often completed in about 2 hours 18 minutes. Hiking around Uztárroz/Uztarroze is defined by mountainous Pyrenean landscapes, extensive forests, and notable gorges and waterfalls. The network offers options for easy walks, moderate trails, and challenging mountaineering routes. The routes in Uztárroz/Uztarroze are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 600 reviews. More than 5,800 hikers have used komoot to explore Uztárroz/Uztarroze's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Uztárroz/Uztarroze?
A: There are over 130 hiking routes in the Uztárroz/Uztarroze area, offering a wide range of options for all skill levels. These include 18 easy trails, 53 moderate routes, and 59 more challenging hikes.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the hiking trails around Uztárroz/Uztarroze?
A: The terrain around Uztárroz/Uztarroze is predominantly mountainous, characterized by the Pyrenean landscape. You'll encounter dense forests, valleys, and significant elevation changes, with some routes leading to high peaks and through gorges.

Q: Are there any family-friendly hiking trails in Uztárroz/Uztarroze?
A: Yes, the region offers several easy trails suitable for families. These routes typically take up to 2 hours with a maximum of 300 meters of elevation gain, making them accessible for all fitness levels. While specific family-friendly routes are not highlighted in the guide, the 18 easy trails provide good options.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see while hiking in Uztárroz/Uztarroze?
A: The Uztárroz/Uztarroze area is rich in natural attractions. You can explore the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/963896" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Holzarte Footbridge&lt;/a&gt; spanning the Olhadubi Gorge, visit the magnificent &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/876139" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Belabarzeko Urjausia - natural swimming pool&lt;/a&gt; waterfall, or ascend to the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2706541" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pic d'Orhy&lt;/a&gt; for panoramic views. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e970293999/artikomendia-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Artikomendia Trail&lt;/a&gt; also features Romanesque bridges and the Cueva del Ibón.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Uztárroz/Uztarroze?
A: The best time for hiking generally depends on the trail's difficulty. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery for most routes. For higher altitude or more challenging trails, summer provides more stable weather. Winter hiking is possible but requires specific gear and experience due to snow conditions in the Pyrenees.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in the Uztárroz/Uztarroze area?
A: Yes, there are several circular routes available. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6979836" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Belabarzeko Urjausia - natural swimming pool – Ateas de Belabarze Pass loop from Belagua&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult circular hike, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/1609090" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ateas de Belabarze Pass – Belabarzeko Urjausia - natural swimming pool loop from Isaba / Izaba&lt;/a&gt; offers a moderate option.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about the trails in Uztárroz/Uztarroze?
A: The trails in Uztárroz/Uztarroze are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 600 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse Pyrenean landscapes, the extensive forests, and the notable gorges and waterfalls. The variety of routes, from easy walks to challenging mountaineering, is also a significant draw.

Q: Are there options for challenging hikes or mountaineering in Uztárroz/Uztarroze?
A: Absolutely. The region is known for its challenging routes, including mountaineering options. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e968755480/pic-d-orhy-trail-from-port-de-larrau" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pic d'Orhy Trail from Port de Larrau&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult ascent to the highest point in the Basque Country. The area has 59 difficult routes, some requiring very good fitness, sturdy shoes, and alpine experience.

Q: Where can I find accommodation or places to eat near the hiking trails?
A: Uztárroz/Uztarroze and the surrounding valleys offer various options. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2709792" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Belagua Refuge&lt;/a&gt; is a significant base for mountaineering, providing accommodation and food services year-round. Other huts and cabins like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2706543" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cabane d'Ardane Gaïnekoa&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4929248" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Zuriza Hostel&lt;/a&gt; are also available. The local towns will also have restaurants and cafes.

Q: Is public transport available to reach trailheads in Uztárroz/Uztarroze?
A: While Uztárroz/Uztarroze is nestled in a natural, mountainous region, public transport options to specific trailheads can be limited. It is advisable to check local bus schedules or consider personal vehicle access for reaching many of the more remote starting points.

Q: Are there any specific rules or considerations for hiking with dogs in Uztárroz/Uztarroze?
A: While many natural areas in Spain are dog-friendly, it's always best to keep dogs on a leash, especially in areas with livestock or protected wildlife. Be mindful of local regulations, particularly in natural parks or reserves, and ensure you clean up after your pet. Water sources might be scarce on some trails, so carry enough for your dog.

Q: Are there any permits required for hiking in the Uztárroz/Uztarroze area?
A: Generally, permits are not required for day hiking on established trails in the Uztárroz/Uztarroze region. However, for specific activities like mountaineering, overnight stays in certain refuges, or accessing highly protected areas, it's always wise to check with local tourist information centers or park authorities for any specific requirements.
tips: The Col de Laza is located in Spain not far from the French border and the port of Larrau. Shorter than its neighbour, its ascent takes place in the middle of a beautiful forest which opens onto lovely views of the surrounding mountains.

The Irati-Abodi Mountain Center is a modern and functional building where you can rent rackets and equipment for Nordic skiing during the winter and bicycles for mountain biking and hiking the rest of the year.
A narrow two-lane road that rises in the upper area with a lot of views over meadow slopes to the rather barren pass. No steep sections, mostly with 7-10%.
